attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who conserves energy by sitting on stronger riders' wheels before soloing or sprinting in final kilometers. When in peak form (2017), capable of attacking from GC group on final climbs to bridge massive gaps.
team_tactics: Minimal team support visible; relies on self-positioning in breakaways. Will let teammates (e.g., ROUSSEAU) work before withholding effort for perfectly-timed late attacks.
terrain_preference: High-altitude climbs (excels above 2600m) and stage-ending ascents where he can time final attacks. Multi-climb stages allow selective KOM point-hunting while managing energy for finale.
vulnerability: Insufficient form for sustained GC-level efforts; immediately dropped by counter-attacks from in-form climbers (2019 Landa example). Relies heavily on breakaway success rather than contesting from favorites' group except when peaking.
key_patterns: Sits on wheels in breakaways, withholds effort until final climbs for solo/sprint finish (4 observations)
Prioritizes KOM jersey mathematics over stage wins when competing for classification (3 observations 2017)
Most dangerous from established breakaways; times final attacks inside 1-3km perfectly (3 wins documented)
Peak climbing form allows bridging multi-minute gaps on final climbs from GC group (2017 only)
High-altitude specialist: specifically targets climbs above 2600m for KOM points and wins (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Recent 2023 Giro win from breakaway confirms continued breakaway threat, but no evidence of 2017 peak form where he attacked GC groups. Operating as specialist rather than GC climber.
